---
title: Advanced Numerical Methods for Materials and Manufacturing -- (in 10 minutes?)
subtitle: Background, Motivation, Path
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    theme: dark
    css: custom.css
engine: knitr
author:
  - name: Andrea Dziubek
    orcid: 0000-0002-7276-8145
    email: dziubea@sunypoly.edu
    affiliations: Department of Mathematics at SUNY Polytechnic Institute 
date: last-modified
---

## {style="margin-top: -40px; font-size: 0.75em"}

[ORCID Profile](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-7276-8145)

- Associate Professor at SUNY Poly
- GMSPD: organizing workshops (2010 - 2025) 
- Few *cool* publications including a book translation (Tensor Analysis)

[SUNY Poly Mathematics Stories](https://www.sunypoly.edu/academics/majors-and-programs/mathematics.html)

- TU Berlin / Germany connection
- Undergraduates: MAT 460, SURP, HRUMC, Independent Study, etc. 
- GMSPD: talks, workshops, conferences, student presentations, etc.

[Personal Website: Research Interests](https://people.sunypoly.edu/~dziubea/)

- Structure Preserving Numerical Methods (Discretizations) = SPD
- Theoretical Mechanics/Physics and Differential Geometry = GM 
- [AI and Robotics (CESSAIR, TDAC)]{.bg style="--col: rgb(94, 11, 113)"} 
    <!--  U.S. Army's Transformation Decision Analysis Center -->
- Modeling and Simulation of PDEs on Curved Surfaces (Opthalmology)

[NGSolve](https://ngsolve.org/) -- an open-source finite element library, with focus on SPD and GM 
([Laplace](https://docu.ngsolve.org/latest/i-tutorials/wta/poisson.html), [motivation](https://www-users.cse.umn.edu/~arnold/disasters/disasters.html))
 


## {style="margin-top: -40px; font-size: 0.75em"}

<h3 style="color: #ff8c00">Structure Preserving Discretizations</h3>

- Addresses two issues in multivariable calculus:
  - The cross product is only defined in $\mathbb{R}^3$
  - A conservative vector field has a potential — the converse requires
    simply connected topology
- Natural next step after Calculus III: exterior calculus and vector-valued differential forms
  - De Rham complex, Hodge decomposition, harmonic forms
- Two main approaches:
  - Discrete Exterior Calculus (DEC) and Finite Element Exterior Calculus (FEEC)

<h3 style="color: #ff8c00">Geometric Mechanics</h3>

- Classical continuum mechanics vs. vector bundle field theories
  (Cosserat, micromorphic, ...)
- Riemannian geometry: mechanics, fluids, materials, EM, GR
- Riemann-Cartan: materials with defects, plasticity, ECT

[Humble Advice](https://www.sunypoly.edu/academics/majors-and-programs/mathematics.html)

- Most important courses: Linear Algebra, Calculus III, Numerical Methods, plus one more CMP 
- Why not double major in Math/Physics and Engineering/Science — often less than one year extra
- Or add a minor in Math, Physics, Applied Computational Math, Finance, ...


## Computational Mathematical Physics = Happiness {style="margin-top: -40px; font-size: 0.75em"}

```{tikz}
%| fig-align: center
%| fig-width: 6
%| fig-height: 6
\usetikzlibrary{backgrounds}
\begin{tikzpicture}[scale=0.6, background rectangle/.style={fill={rgb,255:red,34;green,32;blue,37}}, show background rectangle]

  % define colors for dark background
  \definecolor{brightpurple}{RGB}{200,100,255}
  \definecolor{brightteal}{RGB}{0,210,210}
  \definecolor{brightgreen}{RGB}{0,200,100}

  % outlines
  \draw[thick, brightpurple] (90:1.7)  circle (2.5);
  \draw[thick, brightteal]   (210:1.7) circle (2.5);
  \draw[thick, brightgreen]  (330:1.7) circle (2.5);

  % labels
  \node[font=\bfseries\small, brightpurple] at (90:2.7)  {Comp};
  \node[font=\bfseries\small, brightteal]   at (210:2.7) {Math};
  \node[font=\bfseries\small, brightgreen]  at (330:2.7) {Physics};

  % smiley face in the center
  \draw[yellow, thick] (0,0) circle (0.35);
  \fill[yellow] (-.15, 0.08) circle (0.05);
  \fill[yellow] ( .15, 0.08) circle (0.05);
  \draw[yellow, thick] (-0.15,-0.08) .. controls (-0.15,-0.22) and (0.15,-0.22) .. (0.15,-0.08);

\end{tikzpicture}
```

## Cosserat (Meta) Material {style="margin-top: -40px; font-size: 0.75em"}

```{tikz}
%| fig-align: center
%| fig-width: 8
%| fig-height: 4
\usetikzlibrary{arrows.meta, decorations.markings, backgrounds}
\begin{tikzpicture}[background rectangle/.style={fill={rgb,255:red,34;green,32;blue,37}}, show background rectangle]

  \definecolor{teal}{RGB}{0,210,210}
  \definecolor{purple}{RGB}{200,100,255}
  \definecolor{orange}{RGB}{255,160,50}
  \definecolor{lgray}{RGB}{200,200,200}

  % reference body
  \draw[thick, lgray] (0,0) rectangle (2.5,2.5);
  \node[white] at (1.25, 2.8) {\small Reference $\mathcal{B}_0$};

  % material point with triad
  \fill[white] (1.25,1.25) circle (0.06);
  \draw[->, thick, teal]   (1.25,1.25) -- (2.0, 1.25) node[right, teal]{\small $\mathbf{e}_1$};
  \draw[->, thick, purple] (1.25,1.25) -- (1.25, 2.0) node[above, purple]{\small $\mathbf{e}_2$};

  % arrow to deformed body
  \draw[->, thick, white] (3.0, 1.25) -- (4.2, 1.25) node[midway, above, white]{\small $\varphi$};

  % deformed body
  \draw[thick, lgray] (4.5,0.2) .. controls (5.0,0) and (6.5,0.5) .. (7.0,1.0)
                         .. controls (7.5,1.5) and (7.2,2.8) .. (6.5,2.8)
                         .. controls (5.5,3.0) and (4.5,2.5) .. (4.5,0.2);
  \node[white] at (5.75, 3.2) {\small Deformed $\mathcal{B}$};

  % deformed point with rotated triad
  \fill[white] (5.75,1.5) circle (0.06);
  \draw[->, thick, teal]   (5.75,1.5) -- (6.5,  1.8) node[right, teal]{\small $R\mathbf{e}_1$};
  \draw[->, thick, purple] (5.75,1.5) -- (5.45, 2) node[above, purple]{\small $R\mathbf{e}_2$};

  % rotation arrow
  %\draw[->, thick, orange] (5.75,1.5) arc (0:50:0.5);
  \node[right, orange] at (5, 1) {\small $R \in SO(3)$};
  

  % displacement vector
  \draw[->, dashed, orange] (1.25,1.25) .. controls (2.5,0.5) .. (5.75,1.5)
    node[midway, below, orange]{\small $\mathbf{u}$};

  % label
  \node[white] at (3.5, -0.5) {$(\mathbf{u}, R)$ : displacement + microrotation};

\end{tikzpicture}
```


## Finite Element Triple $(K, P_K, \Sigma_K)$ {style="margin-top: -40px; font-size: 0.75em"}

```{tikz}
%| fig-align: center
%| fig-width: 8
%| fig-height: 4
\usetikzlibrary{backgrounds}
\begin{tikzpicture}[background rectangle/.style={fill={rgb,255:red,34;green,32;blue,37}}, show background rectangle]

  \definecolor{teal}{RGB}{0,210,210}
  \definecolor{purple}{RGB}{200,100,255}
  \definecolor{orange}{RGB}{255,160,50}
  \definecolor{lgray}{RGB}{180,180,180}

  % domain boundary
  \draw[thick, white] 
    (0,0) .. controls (0.5,-0.3) and (3.5,-0.3) .. (4,0)
          .. controls (4.5,0.5) and (4.3,2.5) .. (4,3)
          .. controls (3.5,3.3) and (0.5,3.3) .. (0,3)
          .. controls (-0.3,2.5) and (-0.3,0.5) .. (0,0);

  % triangles - drawn directly with coordinates
  % row 1
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (0.4,0.4) -- (1.3,0.2) -- (1.2,1.1) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (1.3,0.2) -- (2.3,0.3) -- (1.2,1.1) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (2.3,0.3) -- (2.2,1.0) -- (1.2,1.1) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (2.3,0.3) -- (3.3,0.4) -- (2.2,1.0) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (3.3,0.4) -- (3.8,1.2) -- (3.2,1.2) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (3.3,0.4) -- (2.2,1.0) -- (3.2,1.2) -- cycle;

  % row 2
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (0.3,1.3) -- (1.2,1.1) -- (0.4,2.2) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (1.2,1.1) -- (1.3,2.1) -- (0.4,2.2) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (1.2,1.1) -- (2.2,1.0) -- (1.3,2.1) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (2.2,1.0) -- (2.2,2.0) -- (1.3,2.1) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (2.2,1.0) -- (3.2,1.2) -- (2.2,2.0) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (3.2,1.2) -- (3.1,2.1) -- (2.2,2.0) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (3.2,1.2) -- (3.7,2.2) -- (3.1,2.1) -- cycle;

  % row 3
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (0.4,2.2) -- (1.3,2.1) -- (0.5,3.0) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (1.3,2.1) -- (1.4,2.9) -- (0.5,3.0) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (1.3,2.1) -- (2.2,2.0) -- (1.4,2.9) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (2.2,2.0) -- (2.3,2.9) -- (1.4,2.9) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (2.2,2.0) -- (3.1,2.1) -- (2.3,2.9) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (3.1,2.1) -- (3.2,2.8) -- (2.3,2.9) -- cycle;
  \draw[teal, thin, opacity=0.7] (3.1,2.1) -- (3.7,2.2) -- (3.2,2.8) -- cycle;

  % highlight element K
  \fill[teal, opacity=0.15] (1.2,1.1) -- (2.2,1.0) -- (1.3,2.1) -- cycle;
  \draw[orange, thick] (1.2,1.1) -- (2.2,1.0) -- (1.3,2.1) -- cycle;
  \node[orange] at (1.55, 1.45) {\small $K$};

  % nodes - drawn explicitly
  \fill[white] (0.4,0.4) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (1.3,0.2) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (2.3,0.3) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (3.3,0.4) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (3.8,1.2) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (0.3,1.3) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (1.2,1.1) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (2.2,1.0) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (3.2,1.2) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (3.7,2.2) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (0.4,2.2) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (1.3,2.1) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (2.2,2.0) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (3.1,2.1) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (0.5,3.0) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (1.4,2.9) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (2.3,2.9) circle (0.05);
  \fill[white] (3.2,2.8) circle (0.05);

  % Ciarlet node labels
  \node[purple] at (1.05, 1.05) {\small $a_i$};
  \node[purple] at (2.1,  0.85) {\small $a_j$};
  \node[purple] at (1.2,  2.25) {\small $a_k$};

  % domain label
  \node[white] at (2.8, 1.6) {\small $\Omega$};

  % boundary labels
  \node[lgray] at (-0.6, 1.5) {\small $\Gamma_D$};
  \node[lgray] at (4.6,  1.5) {\small $\Gamma_N$};

  % fixed BC left
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,0.4) -- (-0.4,0.55);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,0.4) -- (-0.4,0.25);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,1.3) -- (-0.4,1.45);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,1.3) -- (-0.4,1.15);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,2.2) -- (-0.4,2.35);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,2.2) -- (-0.4,2.05);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,3.0) -- (-0.4,3.15);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.1,3.0) -- (-0.4,2.85);
  \draw[lgray] (-0.4, 0.2) -- (-0.4, 3.1);

  % load arrows right
  \draw[->, thick, purple] (4.8,0.8) -- (4.1,0.8);
  \draw[->, thick, purple] (4.8,1.5) -- (4.1,1.5);
  \draw[->, thick, purple] (4.8,2.2) -- (4.1,2.2);
  \node[purple] at (5.1, 1.5) {\small $\mathbf{f}$};

  % Ciarlet triple
  \node[white] at (2.0, -1) {\small $K = \triangle(a_1, a_2, a_3), \quad P_K = P_1(K), \quad \Sigma_K = \{p(a_1),\, p(a_2),\, p(a_3)\}$};
\end{tikzpicture}
```

- Domain $\Omega$ with boundary $\Gamma_D$​ (Dirichlet) and $\Gamma_N$ (Neumann), and outer loads $\mathbf{f}$

- Linear triangle $P_1$: element geometry $K$, local function space $P_K$, degrees of freedom $\Sigma_K$

- FE space: assemble local triples $(K, P_K, \Sigma_K)$ and enforce continuity across element boundaries

<!--
## Additional theme classes

### Some extra things you can do with the clean theme

Special classes for emphasis

- `.alert` class for default emphasis, e.g. [important note]{.alert}.
- `.fg` class for custom colour, e.g. [important note]{.fg style="--col: #e64173"}.
- `.bg` class for custom background, e.g. [important note]{.bg style="--col: #e64173"}.

Cross-references

- `.button` class provides a Beamer-like button, e.g.
[[Summary]{.button}](#sec-summary)

## Want more?

### See our longer demo slides

We've deliberarely kept this template lean, to get you up and running as fast as
possible.

We provide a separate
[demo template](https://github.com/grantmcdermott/quarto-revealjs-clean-demo),
with more examples for integrating code, tables, figures, etc.

  - See the live demo slides
  [here](https://grantmcdermott.com/quarto-revealjs-clean-demo/template.html).

## Summary {#sec-summary}

### A minimalist and elegant presentation theme

The Quarto reveal.js clean theme aims to be a minimalist and elegant presention
theme. Here are some options to get you started:

Add the theme to an existing project.

```{.bash}
quarto install extension grantmcdermott/quarto-revealjs-clean
```

... or, create a new project using this slide deck as a lean template.

```{.bash}
quarto use template grantmcdermott/quarto-revealjs-clean
```

... or, create a new project using the demo slide deck as a full template.

```{.bash}
quarto use template grantmcdermott/quarto-revealjs-clean-demo
```
-->
